heading: Multiemployer plan benefits guaranteed
label: 1322a Multiemployer plan benefits guaranteed

children.heading: Benefits of covered plans subject to guarantee
children.label: a Benefits of covered plans subject to guarantee

children.text: to which this subchapter applies, and
which is insolvent under section 1426(b) or 1441(d)(2) of this title.

children.heading: Benefits or benefit increases not eligible for guarantee
children.label: b Benefits or benefit increases not eligible for guarantee
A
children.text: For purposes of this section, a benefit or benefit increase which has been in effect under a plan for less than 60 months is not eligible for the corporation’s guarantee. For purposes of this paragraph, any month of any plan year during which the plan was insolvent or terminated (within the meaning of ) shall not be taken into account.section 1341a(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of this section, a benefit or benefit increase which has been in effect under a plan for less than 60 months before the first day of the plan year for which an amendment reducing the benefit or the benefit increase is taken into account under section 1425(a)(2) of this title in determining the minimum contribution requirement for the plan year under section 1423(b) of this title is not eligible for the corporation’s guarantee.11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which the documents establishing or increasing the benefit were executed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the effective date of the benefit or benefit increase;

children.label: B
children.text: the period of time for which a benefit or a benefit increase has been in effect under a successor plan includes the period of time for which the benefit or benefit increase was in effect under a previously established plan; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a plan to which did not apply on , the time periods referred to in this section are computed beginning on the date on which first applies to the plan.section 1321 of this titlesection 1321 of this titleSeptember 3, 1974

children.heading: Determinations respecting amount of guarantee
children.label: c Determinations respecting amount of guarantee
ii
children.text: the accrual rate, if any, in excess of $11, and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of the participant’s years of credited service.

children.label: i
children.text: no greater than the monthly benefit which would be payable under the plan at normal retirement age in the form of a single life annuity, and

children.label: ii
children.text: determined without regard to any reduction under ; divided bysection 411(a)(3)(E) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the participant’s years of credited service.

children.label: i
children.text: a full year of participation in the plan, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any period of service before participation which is credited for purposes of benefit accrual as the equivalent of a full year of participation;

children.label: B
children.text: any year for which the participant is credited for purposes of benefit accrual with a fraction of the equivalent of a full year of participation shall be counted as such a fraction of a year of credited service; and

children.label: C
children.text: years of credited service shall be determined by including service which may otherwise be disregarded by the plan under .section 411(a)(3)(E) of title 26

For purposes of subsection (a), in the case of a qualified preretirement survivor annuity (as defined in ) payable to the surviving spouse of a participant under a multiemployer plan which becomes insolvent under or 1441(d)(2) of this title or is terminated, such annuity shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the participant has not died as of the date on which the plan became so insolvent or the termination date.section 1055(e)(1) of this titlesection 1426(b) of this title

children.heading: Amount of guarantee of reduced benefit
children.label: d Amount of guarantee of reduced benefit

children.text: the reduced benefit, or
the amount determined under subsection (c).

children.heading: Ineligibility of benefits for guarantee
children.label: e Ineligibility of benefits for guarantee
children.text: The corporation shall not guarantee benefits under a multiemployer plan which, under , would not be guaranteed under a single-employer plan.section 1322(b)(6) of this title

children.heading: Study, report, etc., respecting premium increase in existing basic-benefit guarantee levels; Congressional procedures applicable for revision of schedules
children.label: f Study, report, etc., respecting premium increase in existing basic-benefit guarantee levels; Congressional procedures applicable for revision of schedules
i
children.text: the premiums needed to maintain the basic-benefit guarantee levels for multiemployer plans described in subsection (c), and

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the basic-benefit guarantee levels for multiemployer plans may be increased without increasing the basic-benefit premiums for multiemployer plans under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: report such determinations to the Committee on Ways and Means and the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and to the Committee on Finance and the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: a revised schedule of basic-benefit guarantees for multiemployer plans which would be necessary in the absence of an increase in premiums approved in accordance with ,section 1306(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a revised schedule of basic-benefit premiums for multiemployer plans which is necessary to support the existing basic-benefit guarantees for such plans, and

children.label: iii
children.text: a revised schedule of basic-benefit guarantees for multiemployer plans for which the schedule of premiums necessary is higher than the existing premium schedule for such plans but lower than the revised schedule of premiums for such plans specified in clause (ii), together with such schedule of premiums.

children.label: B
children.text: The revised schedule of increased premiums referred to in subparagraph (A)(ii) or (A)(iii) shall go into effect as approved by the enactment of a joint resolution.

children.label: C
children.text: If an increase in premiums is not so enacted, the revised guarantee schedule described in subparagraph (A)(i) shall go into effect on the first day of the second calendar year following the year in which such revised guarantee schedule was submitted to the Congress.

children.label: i
children.text: a revised schedule of increases in the basic-benefit guarantees which can be supported by the existing schedule of basic-benefit premiums for multiemployer plans, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a revised schedule of basic-benefit premiums sufficient to support the existing basic-benefit guarantees.

children.label: B
children.text: The revised schedules referred to in subparagraph (A)(i) or subparagraph (A)(ii) shall go into effect as approved by the enactment of a joint resolution.

children.label: A
children.text: The succeeding subparagraphs of this paragraph are enacted by the Congress as an exercise of the rulemaking power of the Senate and the House of Representatives, respectively, and as such they shall be deemed a part of the rules of each House, respectively, but applicable only with respect to the procedure to be followed in that House in the case of joint resolutions (as defined in subparagraph (B)). Such subparagraphs shall supersede other rules only to the extent that they are inconsistent therewith. They are enacted with full recognition of the constitutional right of either House to change the rules (so far as relating to the procedure of that House) at any time, in the same manner, and to the same extent as in the case of any rule of that House.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, “joint resolution” means only a joint resolution, the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “The proposed schedule described in transmitted to the Congress by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation on is hereby approved.”, the first blank space therein being filled with “section 4022A(f)(2)(A)(ii) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974”, “section 4022A(f)(2)(A)(iii) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974”, “section 4022A(f)(3)(A)(i) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974”, or “section 4022A(f)(3)(A)(ii) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974” (whichever is applicable), and the second blank space therein being filled with the date on which the corporation’s message proposing the revision was submitted.

children.label: C
children.text: The procedure for disposition of a joint resolution shall be the procedure described in section 1306(b)(4) through (7) of this title.

children.heading: Guarantee of payment of other classes of benefits and establishment of terms and conditions of guarantee; promulgation of regulations for establishment of supplemental program to guarantee benefits otherwise ineligible; status of benefits; applicability of revised schedule of premiums
children.label: g Guarantee of payment of other classes of benefits and establishment of terms and conditions of guarantee; promulgation of regulations for establishment of supplemental program to guarantee benefits otherwise ineligible; status of benefits; applicability of revised schedule of premiums

children.text: The corporation may guarantee the payment of such other classes of benefits under multiemployer plans, and establish the terms and conditions under which those other classes of benefits are guaranteed, as it determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: The corporation shall prescribe regulations to establish a supplemental program to guarantee benefits under multiemployer plans which would be guaranteed under this section but for the limitations in subsection (c). Such regulations shall be proposed by the corporation no later than the end of the 18th calendar month following . The regulations shall make coverage under the supplemental program available no later than . Any election to participate in the supplemental program shall be on a voluntary basis, and a plan electing such coverage shall continue to pay the premiums required under to the revolving fund used pursuant to in connection with benefits otherwise guaranteed under this section. Any such election shall be irrevocable, except to the extent otherwise provided by regulations prescribed by the corporation.September 26, 1980January 1, 1983section 1306(a)(2)(B) of this titlesection 1305 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: that a plan must elect coverage under the supplemental program within the time permitted by the regulations;

children.label: ii
children.text: unless the corporation determines otherwise, that a plan may not elect supplemental coverage unless the value of the assets of the plan as of the end of the plan year preceding the plan year in which the election must be made is an amount equal to 15 times the total amount of the benefit payments made under the plan for that year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such other reasonable terms and conditions for supplemental coverage, including funding standards and any other reasonable limitations with respect to plans or benefits covered or to means of program financing, as the corporation determines are necessary and appropriate for a feasible supplemental program consistent with the purposes of this subchapter.
Any benefits guaranteed under this subsection shall be considered nonbasic benefits for purposes of this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: the revised schedule is submitted to the Congress, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a joint resolution described in subparagraph (B) is not enacted before the close of the 60th legislative day after such schedule is submitted to the Congress.

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a joint resolution described in this subparagraph is a joint resolution the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “The revised premium schedule transmitted to the Congress by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation under section 4022A(g)(4) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 on is hereby disapproved.”, the blank space therein being filled with the date on which the revised schedule was submitted.

children.label: C
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “legislative day” means any calendar day other than a day on which either House is not in session because of a sine die adjournment or an adjournment of more than 3 days to a day certain.

children.label: D
children.text: The procedure for disposition of a joint resolution described in subparagraph (B) shall be the procedure described in paragraphs (4) through (7) of .section 1306(b) of this title

Regulations prescribed by the corporation to carry out the provisions of this subsection, may, to the extent provided therein, supersede the requirements of sections 1426, 1431, and 1441 of this title, and the requirements of , but only with respect to benefits guaranteed under this subsection.section 418E of title 26

children.heading: Applicability to nonforfeitable benefits accrued as of ; manner and extent of guaranteeJuly 30, 1980
children.label: h Applicability to nonforfeitable benefits accrued as of ; manner and extent of guaranteeJuly 30, 1980

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), subsections (b) and (c) shall not apply with respect to the nonforfeitable benefits accrued as of , with respect to a participant or beneficiary under a multiemployer plan—July 29, 1980
who is within 36 months of the normal retirement age and has a nonforfeitable right to a pension as of that date.3So in original. Probably should be “(B)”.3

The benefits described in paragraph (1) shall be guaranteed by the corporation in the same manner and to the same extent as benefits are guaranteed by the corporation under (without regard to this section).section 1322 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in which the plan has terminated within the meaning of , orsection 1341a(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in which it is determined by the corporation that substantially all the employers have withdrawn from the plan pursuant to an agreement or arrangement to withdraw.
